_metadata.title: ComplianceLint Interim Standard for EU AI Act Article 86: Right to Explanation
_metadata.disclaimer: This is an interim standard developed by ComplianceLint. It will be superseded when CEN-CENELEC publishes the harmonized standard for AI transparency and explainability.
basis: EU AI Act Recitals 171-173 (legislative intent on right to explanation)
_metadata.awaiting_standard: CEN-CENELEC harmonized standard for AI transparency and explainability (expected Q4 2026)

requirements.title: Explanation Capability
requirements.requirement: The deployer MUST be able to provide clear and meaningful explanations of the role of the AI system in the decision-making procedure and the main elements of the decision taken, upon request by affected persons.
requirements.rationale: Art. 86(1) grants affected persons the right to obtain explanations of AI-assisted decisions.
required_content: Explanation of what role the AI system played in the decision
Main elements and factors that influenced the decision
How the AI output was used by the deployer
Whether and how human review was applied
How to contest or seek review of the decision

requirements.title: Explanation Delivery Mechanism
requirements.requirement: The deployer MUST have a mechanism for affected persons to request and receive explanations in a clear and meaningful manner.
requirements.rationale: Art. 86(1) requires explanations to be obtainable, implying a functional delivery mechanism.
acceptable_implementations: Request process with defined response timeline
Automated explanation generation (SHAP, LIME, feature importance)
Contact point or form for explanation requests

requirements.title: Exception Analysis
requirements.requirement: Where the deployer claims an exception under Art. 86(2) or (3), the deployer MUST document the legal basis for the exception.
requirements.rationale: Art. 86(2)-(3) provide exceptions, but the deployer must justify their applicability.
acceptable_implementations: Legal analysis documenting applicable Union or national law exceptions
Written opinion from legal counsel on exception applicability
Cross-reference to other Union law providing equivalent right to explanation
